title: athyrium filix-femina subsp. pectinatum
intro: Athyrium filix-femina subsp. pectinatum, also known as lady fern, is a subspecies of fern native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive fronds with pectinate (comb-like) margins. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Athyrium filix-femina subsp. pectinatum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
